title: cowallet Next.js Landing Page
description: Build a product landing page using Next.js App Router + Tailwind CSS with embedded interactive phone simulator from prototype

stories.title: Project scaffolding and configuration
acceptanceCriteria: website/ directory exists with package.json containing next@15, react@19, tailwindcss@4
next.config.ts has output: 'export' for static generation
tailwind.config.ts extends theme with all design tokens (paper, ink, accent, etc.)
src/app/globals.css imports tailwind and defines CSS variables matching prototype
npm run build completes without errors
npm run dev starts dev server successfully on port 3000

stories.title: Language context and i18n system
acceptanceCriteria: src/context/LangContext.tsx exports LangProvider and useLang hook
useLang returns { lang, setLang, t } where t(key) resolves bilingual strings
src/lib/i18n.ts contains all landing page copy in zh and en
Language defaults to zh and persists to localStorage on change
TypeScript compiles with no errors

stories.title: Shared UI components (Orb, Button, LangToggle)
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/ui/Orb.tsx renders the SVG orb with breathe animation, accepts size prop
src/components/ui/Button.tsx supports variants: primary, accent, outline, ghost with proper styles
src/components/ui/LangToggle.tsx toggles between EN/中 using LangContext
All components render without errors in the dev server

stories.title: Layout and Navbar
acceptanceCriteria: src/app/layout.tsx provides LangProvider wrapper and font loading
Navbar is fixed top with cowallet.ai logo + mini orb on left, LangToggle + CTA on right
Navbar has backdrop-blur glass effect on scroll
Page renders at localhost:3000 with navbar visible

stories.title: Hero section with phone simulator placeholder
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/sections/Hero.tsx renders headline, subtitle, CTA buttons
Desktop layout: left text / right placeholder for phone simulator
Mobile layout: stacked, text above, placeholder below
Background uses radial-gradient warm paper texture from prototype
All text is bilingual via t() function

stories.title: Features section
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/sections/Features.tsx renders 3 feature cards in grid
Each card has colored icon, title, description matching prototype content
3 columns on desktop, stacks to single column on mobile
All text bilingual

stories.title: Security section (Three Keys)
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/sections/Security.tsx renders centered headline + 3 key cards
Each card: icon + title + location + status badge (green/amber)
Below cards: plain-language explanation paragraph
Tech detail card for pros with MPC 2-of-3 info
All text bilingual

stories.title: AI Showcase section
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/sections/AiShowcase.tsx renders 3 scenarios in alternating layout
Each scenario has text description + static phone mockup component
Static phone mockups use PhoneFrame shell with frozen view state inside
Responsive: side-by-side on desktop, stacked on mobile
All text bilingual

stories.title: Protocols, CTA, and Footer sections
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/sections/Protocols.tsx lists MCP, x402, REST, Webhook, EIP-6963 with LIVE badges and A2A as soon
src/components/sections/Cta.tsx renders headline + download buttons (placeholder) + orb
src/components/sections/Footer.tsx renders copyright, tagline, placeholder links, LangToggle
All text bilingual

stories.title: Phone simulator - frame and state management
acceptanceCriteria: src/hooks/usePhoneState.ts exports hook managing currentView, onboardingStage, showOnboarding, userName, chatMessages, attachedImg
src/components/phone/PhoneFrame.tsx renders phone bezel, notch, home indicator matching prototype CSS
src/styles/phone.css contains extracted prototype styles scoped under .phone-scope
PhoneFrame renders correctly in dev server with proper styling

stories.title: Phone simulator - Onboarding flow
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/phone/Onboarding.tsx implements all 8 stages: hero, start, creating, importing, bio, name, ready, persona
Stage transitions work via state changes with CSS animations
Creating stage shows progress animation (key splitting)
Bio stage shows Face ID scan animation
Name stage accepts input
Completing onboarding reveals main app views

stories.title: Phone simulator - Main views (Home, Wallet, Agents, Settings, Keys)
acceptanceCriteria: HomeView.tsx renders status, greeting, balance, quick actions, try-prompts, activity feed, showcase cards
WalletView.tsx renders balance summary, asset list, securities, yield card
AgentsView.tsx renders agents hero, connected agents, skills grid, protocols list
SettingsView.tsx renders security section, conversation settings, general settings
KeysView.tsx renders three-key detail with status cards and explanation
Tab bar navigation switches between views

stories.title: Phone simulator - Chat and intent recognition
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/intents.ts exports INTENT_RULES with regex patterns from prototype
ChatView.tsx renders empty state with suggestions, message thread, intent cards
Sending a message matching an intent shows the intent confirmation card
Intent card has yes/no buttons that produce follow-up responses
Composer with text input, image attach button, voice button, send button all render

stories.title: Phone simulator - Auto-demo controller
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/phone/DemoController.tsx orchestrates automatic walkthrough
Demo button visible above phone frame triggers the demo
Demo walks through onboarding, home, chat intent, wallet, agents, settings in sequence
Demo can be stopped mid-run via exit button
Demo cursor and caption overlays render correctly

stories.title: Integration - Hero embeds live phone simulator
acceptanceCriteria: PhoneDemo.tsx wraps the full phone simulator with dynamic import (no SSR)
Hero section renders the interactive phone simulator on the right (desktop) or below (mobile)
Phone simulator and landing page share LangContext - toggling language updates both
npm run build (static export) succeeds with the full page
Page loads in browser with all sections visible and phone simulator interactive
